Name: ANA Restaurant
Intro: Concept & Interior Design, Brand Experience, Customer Journey, Menu & Experience Design
Top Section - Text #1: ANA was conceived as a living cultural canvas, reflecting the UAE’s diversity and Emirati hospitality. The façade features a “no-logo logo” wall, where every version of “ANA / أنا” represents a different personality and identity. This symbolism mirrors the UAE as a melting pot of cultures, translated into Emirati-fusion cuisine, interior design, and a holistic guest experience. The design balances contemporary Arabesque aesthetics with modern luxury — refined materials, warm tones, and bold art statements. From dining areas to the majlis event zone, every detail reinforces ANA’s philosophy of celebrating individuality through shared experience.
Top Section - Text #2:
ANA was designed as an immersive cultural statement, not just a restaurant. The façade’s “no-logo logo” wall reflects identity as multiplicity — each reinterpretation of ANA echoing the diversity of the UAE. Inside, the space becomes a modern majlīs where Emirati heritage and contemporary design fuse seamlessly.

The interiors balance minimal geometry with bold artistic gestures: sculptural masks, reflective installations, and rhythmic lighting turn dining into a theatrical experience. Materials — polished stone, warm upholstery, textured walls — evoke both modern sophistication and the intimacy of Emirati hospitality. From tableware and uniforms to curated event spaces, every detail was conceived to translate ANA’s philosophy: a fusion of food, culture, and belonging.

‍

I envisioned and directed ANA’s concept and design from A to Z, ensuring the brand philosophy extended seamlessly across space, experience, and storytelling. My contributions included:

  • Led the concept creation and interior design vision from start to finish.
  • Directed the façade design, including the “no-logo logo” wall.
  • Selected materials, finishes, and furniture to reflect the fusion of heritage and modernity.
  • Designed uniforms, table setups, plates, tableware, and cutlery to align with the brand identity.
  • Shaped the customer experience and communication style across touchpoints.
  • Developed the menu design and structure, including naming hero dishes and crafting their stories.
  • Curated decor, display, and visual merchandising to enhance the dining experience.
  • Ensured the integration of cultural storytelling throughout the space, from dining areas to majlis events.

‍

Name: Abu Dhabi Art Fair
Top Section - Text #1: At Abu Dhabi Art Fair, Hind Al-Oud revealed Ahojas through an immersive installation that transformed fragrance into art. Designed as a sculptural, multi-sensory environment, the space invited visitors to think, feel, and create, positioning perfume as both an emotional journey and a contemporary artwork.
Top Section - Text #2:
The installation was conceived as a journey of thought, feeling, and creation — a spatial narrative inspired by the idea “look backward in order to see forward.” The design translated fragrance into an art piece, inviting visitors to move through zones marked Think, Feel, Create.

  • Think: A minimal entry anchored by reflective geometry, prompting visitors to question and explore their needed emotions.
  • Feel: A central immersive feature, layered with textures, light, and cosmic imagery, designed to trigger deeper emotional resonance.
  • Create: An interactive zone where visitors engaged with perfume through sensory touchpoints, connecting with Ahojas as a personal expression of creativity and memory.

Stone textures, glowing spheres, and reflective surfaces merged to create a sculptural, immersive environment, making fragrance a medium of art and interaction.

I led the concept development and spatial design, ensuring the installation positioned Hind Al-Oud within the contemporary art dialogue. My role included:

  • Translating the Ahojas narrative into a multi-sensory installation.
  • Designing the spatial journey (Think, Feel, Create) as a layered emotional experience.
  • Directing the materiality and lighting to create a balance between minimalism and theatrical impact.
  • Supervising execution to ensure the final installation embodied the brand’s vision of perfume as an art form.

The project established Hind Al-Oud as a cultural innovator, bridging the world of luxury perfumery with contemporary art and elevating Ahojas into more than a fragrance — an immersive emotional statement.

Name: Anfasic Flagship Store
Intro: Flagship Store | Luxury Retail Architecture | Rebranding Rollout
Top Section - Headline #1: Designing the Future of Emirati Perfumery
Top Section - Text #1: The Anfasic flagship at Dubai Mall reimagines traditional perfume retail into a futuristic, immersive environment. Curved forms, glowing violet tones, and reflective surfaces create a space that feels both rooted in Emirati culture and ahead of its time. This store became the first physical expression of Anfasic’s rebranding, a bold step into modern luxury design within one of the world’s most prestigious retail destinations.
Top Section - Text #2:
The store was conceived as a multi-sensory journey, where architecture and atmosphere enhance the discovery of fragrance. Sculptural niches highlight collections like art installations, while interactive perfume-testing displays bring technology into the retail experience. Metallic finishes and luminous textures evoke the flow of smoke and scent, reinforcing Anfasic’s heritage in dukhoon and bukhoor while presenting it through a futuristic lens. The spatial language is fluid and immersive, designed to transform every touchpoint into a moment of storytelling.

I directed the creative rebranding vision and translated it into a comprehensive retail concept. My responsibilities included defining the new look and feel, developing design studies, and setting the requirements for zoning, circulation, and customer experience. I created the material palette and product display guidelines, and worked closely with the designers team to ensure the design direction was executed with precision. From concept through to handover, I ensured that every element represented the modernized Anfasic identity and delivered a high-end customer experience.

‍

Name: CHARLES ZUBER
Top Section - Headline #1: Framing Watches as Works of Art
Top Section - Text #1: The Charles Zuber boutique at Yas Mall marks the brand’s first global flagship outside Switzerland. Designed at the Geneva headquarters, the Abu Dhabi boutique was envisioned as a contemporary temple of watchmaking and jewelry, blending Swiss precision with a Middle Eastern sense of luxury.
Top Section - Text #2:
The design embodies “the provocative beauty of simplicity” through arched façades, warm lighting, and bespoke materiality. The façade establishes a dialogue between heritage and modernity, while the interiors showcase meticulous craftsmanship — pearl- and silver-infused flooring, customized finishes, and tailored furniture. Each detail frames the maison’s creations as timeless works of art.

‍

While the architectural design was developed in Switzerland, my responsibility was to translate the brand’s vision into reality in the UAE with an uncompromising focus on luxury quality. My scope included:

  • Supervising execution and ensuring the Geneva design intent was fully realized on site.
  • Selecting and approving high-end materials, finishes, and furniture, with a focus on bespoke elements.
  • Directing the customization of key finishes, including pearl-and-silver flooring produced exclusively for this project.
  • Designing and arranging the product merchandising to enhance storytelling and elevate customer journeys.
  • Guaranteeing consistency of quality across construction, décor, and final handover to meet global luxury standards.

‍

Name: CUD Auditorium
Top Section - Text #1: For Canadian University Dubai, a new auditorium and innovation hub was designed to serve as both the university’s formal ceremonial hall and a training ground for the next generation of marketers and brand builders. With a capacity of 200–300, the space embodies a futuristic, VIP atmosphere aligned with the university’s innovative spirit.
Top Section - Text #2:
The project brought together two distinct yet complementary environments:

  • Auditorium → A landmark venue for graduation ceremonies, official events, and guest lectures featuring regional and international leaders. Designed with sweeping curves, immersive lighting, and refined materials, it conveys prestige while offering flexibility for large-scale events.
  • Workshop & Training Rooms → Interactive spaces for brand engagement, product testing, and marketing simulations, tailored to Gen Z students. Here, global brands can showcase products while students practice product development, storytelling, and campaign strategies.

Together, these spaces reflect the university’s progressive identity, providing students with environments that are both inspirational and functional. The design balances futurism and luxury, creating a cultural anchor for City Walk and a new creative platform for CUD.

‍

I was responsible for the concept development and spatial design, ensuring both programmatic and experiential needs were fulfilled. My scope included:

  • Translating the university’s innovative ethos into an architectural language.
  • Designing the auditorium as a formal yet contemporary space for high-profile events.
  • Creating workshops and training rooms where brands and students interact dynamically.
  • Ensuring the design offered a VIP atmosphere while maintaining functionality for daily use.
  • Overseeing the creative process and guiding the team to deliver a cohesive, futuristic environment.

The project set a new standard for educational environments in Dubai, positioning Canadian University Dubai as a leader in bridging academia, industry, and design innovation.

Name: Dubai Design Week 2024
Intro: Experiential Pop-Up | Artistic Installation | Brand Activation
Top Section - Text #1: At Dubai Design Week, Hind Al-Oud presented an immersive pop-up installation that transformed fragrance into art and poetry into space. Inspired by Kibriya’E by poet Faisal Al-Adwani, the activation introduced two exclusive perfumes — Alifi and Ya’E — expressed as a multi-sensory journey from certainty to singularity, blending scent, light, sound, and spatial storytelling into an evocative design experience.
Top Section - Text #2:
The space was conceived as a multi-sensory journey, guiding visitors from certainty to singularity. Through art, interactive installations, fragrance, soundscapes, and tactile elements, guests experienced an emotional transformation that mirrored the essence of the two perfumes — Alifi, representing ambition and certainty, and Ya’E, celebrating singularity and self-expression.

Minimalist yet immersive, the design elevated fragrance into a narrative-driven environment. Light and visual compositions represented stability evolving into uniqueness, while scent interactions, music, and subtle taste elements deepened the sensory engagement. The result was not just a retail activation, but a creative encounter positioning Hind Al-Oud within Dubai’s vibrant design community, connecting the brand to artists, innovators, and luxury audiences who value emotional storytelling and cultural expression.

As Creative Director, I developed the concept, mapped the customer journey, and directed the spatial and sensory design. My focus was on translating poetry into space, ensuring each touchpoint embodied the transformation from certainty to singularity. I oversaw the design language, immersive interactions, and brand storytelling, ensuring the pop-up resonated as both a luxury experience and a creative statement within the global design stage.

‍

Name: Emovation LAB
Location: Sharjah Research Technology and Innovation Park, UAE
Top Section - Text #1: The Emovation Lab was designed as a first-of-its-kind hub for emotional innovation in Sharjah’s Technology and Innovation Park. Created as a space where invention begins with emotion, it offered a multi-sensory environment for students, researchers, and creators to imagine, develop, and showcase new ideas.
Top Section - Text #2:
The design was rooted in the concept of Emovation — Emotion + Innovation. The Lab unfolded as a circular journey: immersive zones to trigger the senses, minimalist research spaces equipped for experimentation, and showcase areas to present and celebrate new inventions. Minimalist, futuristic, and refined, the architecture combined light, sound, and materiality to make the space both inspiring and functional.

As Creative Director, I conceived and led the project from concept to delivery — studying the site, defining the needs of its users, designing the spatial journey, and integrating storytelling into each touchpoint. I oversaw the interior architecture and execution in detail, ensuring the Lab achieved its luxury and futuristic vision. The result was a pioneering model for innovation spaces, where design itself acts as a catalyst for creativity and invention.

‍

Name: Emovation LAB - Pop-Up Store
Top Section - Text #1: The Emovation Lab was a pioneering multi-sensory pop-up, born from the fusion of emotion and innovation. It transformed fragrance into an immersive journey, redefining how customers engage with luxury retail.
Top Section - Text #2:
The Lab unfolded as a three-step narrative — Interaction, Processing, Reaction — guiding visitors through discovery, experimentation, and creation. Light, sound, reflection, and tactile materials shaped the atmosphere, while the Mixology Lab invited customers to craft their own perfumes. Each element acted as a storytelling trigger, turning the space into a stage where design, technology, and emotion converged.

As Creative Director, I originated the Emovation concept and developed its translation into space, narrative, and interaction. I led the spatial design, customer journey mapping, and integration of storytelling across every touchpoint. My scope included the visual identity, packaging, and digital platforms, ensuring a unified experience across physical and digital realms.

I directed the rollout across GCC, and later at international showcases such as World Beauty and Esxence in Italy.

The Lab became a benchmark in experiential retail design, pioneering a new model of multi-sensory engagement that positioned luxury perfumery as an emotional, participatory experience.

Name: HOB Department Store
Intro: Department Store – Retail Architecture & Multi-Brand Concept
Top Section - Headline #1: The First Emirati Luxury Department Store Reimagined
Top Section - Text #1: HOB marked the UAE’s first Emirati luxury department store, and this Yas Mall flagship was a pivotal moment in elevating its identity. The store was envisioned as a premium multi-brand destination — housing perfumes, fashion, accessories, jewelry, and private event spaces — designed to reflect global luxury standards while remaining rooted in regional identity.
Top Section - Text #2:
The design introduced a contemporary luxury environment defined by high-end finishes, bespoke materials, and flexible layouts to accommodate multiple brands under one roof.

  • Iconic Entrance: The façade features a custom-designed green resin portal, created exclusively for HOB, establishing a bold and recognizable landmark within Yas Mall.
  • Spatial Layout: The floorplan was developed to adapt to diverse brand requirements while ensuring a cohesive flow and premium customer journey.
  • Materials & Atmosphere: A blend of polished marble, metallic detailing, warm lighting, and curated display systems created an immersive luxury retail ambiance.
  • Multi-Brand Identity: Each brand retained visibility and individuality while existing within a unified luxury environment, guided by clear architectural and VM standards.
  • VIP Lounges & Private Areas: Dedicated spaces for high-profile customers and events reinforced exclusivity and elevated brand perception.

I led the creative direction, spatial concept, and retail design guidelines for the store:

  • Developed the overall design concept and customer journey.
  • Directed the layout planning to ensure flexibility for future brand rotations.
  • Created the look & feel, material palette, and finishes — including the exclusive resin façade.
  • Provided design guidelines for in-store brands, ensuring alignment with HOB’s luxury standards.
  • Oversaw the development of bespoke retail units (leather goods, niche brands).
  • Shaped the visual merchandising and activation standards to maintain premium daily operations.

This project redefined HOB’s positioning — transforming it from a retail space into a landmark Emirati luxury department store with global relevance.

Name: Hind Al Oud Pop-Up Store
Top Section - Text #1: This immersive pop-up was designed as a luxury pavilion in the heart of Galleria Mall, combining bold architecture with sensorial storytelling to create a memorable retail destination.
Top Section - Text #2:
The space blended glass, crystal, marble, and stone to establish a striking presence in one of Abu Dhabi’s most prestigious malls. Designed as a journey, the layout guided customers through interactive perfume-testing counters and storytelling zones, each touchpoint revealing a new layer of the brand’s narrative. Light, reflection, and materiality were choreographed to elevate the atmosphere, ensuring that the pop-up didn’t just display products, but delivered an immersive brand story that engaged visitors emotionally as well as visually.

I led the concept creation and interior architecture, directing the project from design through execution. My work included material selection, spatial planning, and storytelling integration across customer touchpoints — from visual merchandising to the immersive testing experience. I supervised site delivery to guarantee high-end finishes, and oversaw the launch presentation, ensuring every detail aligned with the vision of a luxury, story-driven brand experience.

Name: Hind Aloud Flagship Store
Top Section - Headline #1: The First Flagship of a Global Rollout
Top Section - Text #1: The Hind Al-Oud Bas Mall flagship marked the beginning of a full rebranding journey, setting a new benchmark for luxury perfume retail. Designed as an immersive environment where architecture, scent, and storytelling converge, it became the first store in a global rollout that now extends across the UAE, GCC, Moscow, and London.
Top Section - Text #2:
The new concept redefined the brand through emotions as architecture. The store unfolded like a museum of emotions, blending spacious, futuristic forms with luxury materials such as quartz, marble, and reflective surfaces. Minimalist curves and jewel-like interiors embodied duality — stability on the outside, singularity within.

Every element was crafted to elevate fragrance into an artful, multi-sensory experience. Interactive testers, digital storytelling, and unique perfuming stations engaged customers beyond transaction, while spatial sequencing ensured a journey of discovery that merged luxury retail with cultural expression.

‍

As Creative Director, I led the concept study, spatial design, and execution, translating Hind Al-Oud’s philosophy of “scenting new emotions beyond” into an architectural language. My responsibilities included defining the narrative, designing the flow and merchandising, integrating interactive touchpoints, and overseeing material and finish selections.

This flagship not only embodied the brand’s transformation but also became the prototype for all future Hind Al-Oud stores worldwide. Its success established a replicable design system — now rolled out across the UAE and GCC and transferred internationally to Moscow and London — positioning Hind Al-Oud as a pioneer of experiential luxury retail on a global stage.

‍

Name: Innovation Conference Hall
Intro: Corporate Interior | Conference Hall | Experiential Design
Top Section - Text #1: The Innovation Conference Hall was conceived as a prestigious space for VIP gatherings, strategic meetings, and high-level discussions. Designed with a futuristic minimalist language and luxurious detailing, the hall embodies authority, sophistication, and forward-looking vision, creating an environment where leadership and innovation converge.
Top Section - Text #2:
The journey begins with a striking façade wrapped in metallic mesh, announcing the hall’s presence with architectural boldness. A reception and hospitality area welcomes guests into a refined waiting space before they transition into the central hall. Inside, a custom-designed U-shaped conference table integrates advanced digital screens and projection systems, anchoring the room as both a functional and symbolic centerpiece.

The interior combines soft curves, warm gold accents, and natural stone finishes, creating a timeless yet futuristic aesthetic. Custom furniture and integrated displays were designed exclusively for this project, reinforcing its unique identity. Every element, from lighting to zoning, reflects flexibility, authority, and the cultural prestige of its distinguished audience.

I directed the concept and interior architecture, creating a design vision that balanced innovation with luxury. My responsibilities included spatial planning, customer journey flow, material and finish selection, and custom furniture design. I also guided the design studies, detailing, and site supervision to ensure every detail matched the project’s strategic purpose. The result is a bespoke conference hall that elevates the experience of leadership gatherings into an immersive and memorable environment.

Name: MHG Headquarter
Intro: Corporate Headquarters | Experiential Office Design | Emotional Spatial Concept
Top Section - Text #1: The MHG Headquarters was envisioned as more than a workplace — it was designed as an emotional journey. I led the project from concept to completion, shaping the headquarters around the philosophy of Breath Out, Breath In, Innovation. Each zone was crafted to influence mood, wellbeing, and creativity, turning the office into a living ecosystem of energy and belonging.
Top Section - Text #2:
Concept & Design

  • Breath Out Zone: An entry space anchored by the interactive Breath of Light chandelier, where employees release negativity. This area houses HR, Finance, Legal, IT, and Audit — departments that embody stability.
  • Breath In Zone: A creative hub filled with natural light, greenery, organic sofas, and reflective surfaces. This area supports Marketing, Graphics, CRM, VM, Product, and Design teams, inspiring positivity and innovation.
  • Innovation Zone: The heart of leadership and invention, integrating the CEO Office, Perfume Lab, and Admin functions. Open, futuristic, and biophilic, it is designed for future planning and creative breakthroughs.

Design Details

  • Materiality: Light woods, textured white walls, terrazzo, polished concrete, soft fabrics, and abundant plants.
  • Furniture: Custom-designed workstations, modular platforms, beanbags, hanging chairs, and organic sofas.
  • Lighting: A layered scheme with the Breath of Light chandelier as centerpiece, spreading positivity across the space.
  • Spatial Flow: Curved forms and open layouts designed to encourage flexibility, collaboration, and wellbeing.

My Role

I envisioned the concept, developed the full design narrative, and directed every stage — from spatial planning to material and furniture selection. I led the creative direction, interior architecture, and detailing, ensuring the headquarters embodied the philosophy of emotional design. Every finish, fixture, and piece of furniture was chosen under my leadership to translate the concept into reality.

The MHG Headquarters redefined the corporate office as an immersive, emotional, and future-forward workplace — one that inspires people to breathe, create, and innovate.

Name: Private Office
Intro: Private CEO Office | Perfume Lab | Luxury Interior Design
Top Section - Text #1: This CEO office was designed as both an executive headquarters and a creative laboratory, reflecting the dual role of a leader who drives a luxury retail group while personally creating fragrances. The space delivers a first impression of prestige and refinement, while offering a calm, home-like atmosphere rarely found in corporate environments.
Top Section - Text #2:
The design vision was to create an environment that is minimal, elegant, and immersive, reflecting the luxury retail DNA of the client’s business. Every zone of the office was strategically planned to serve a distinct function while maintaining a consistent atmosphere of exclusivity.

  • Executive Office → A refined, minimal layout anchored by a sculptural desk and a backdrop of marble stone with golden veining. Soft fluted wood panels wrap the space, combining warmth and acoustical comfort with vertical rhythm.
  • Perfume Lab → Positioned adjacent to the office, the lab allows the CEO to engage in fragrance creation. Minimal yet functional counters, custom shelving, and carefully lit displays support experimentation while presenting the perfumes as art pieces.
  • Meeting & Boardroom → Defined by the iconic circular table, symbolizing unity and leadership. Neutral tones, soft fabrics, and integrated technology create a seamless environment for both strategic discussions and creative exchange.
  • Hospitality & Workshop Zone → A welcoming waiting area and an interactive bar counter designed for workshops with partner brands. Illuminated shelving units showcase iconic perfumes, merging hospitality with storytelling.

Materiality & Detailing

  • Natural Stone: Hand-selected marble with dramatic veining and perfect pattern alignment, used as a statement wall in executive and meeting zones.
  • Wood: Rippled and fluted natural wood panels provide warmth, depth, and bespoke craftsmanship.
  • Metal Accents: Brushed gold details integrated into furniture, shelving, and lighting elements highlight the luxury narrative.
  • Furniture: All seating and tables were custom-designed and produced specifically for this office, from premium upholstered lounge pieces to tailored executive desks.
  • Lighting: Suspended circular luminaires and concealed LED profiles create layered, atmospheric light that accentuates material textures and enhances the serene mood.
  • Accessories & Styling: Curated décor, plants, and objects were selected to soften the environment and deliver a home-like calmness without compromising prestige.

My Role

As Creative Director, I developed the overall concept, interior design, and furniture direction, translating the CEO’s dual identity as a leader and perfumer into space. My role included:

  • Designing the spatial flow and zoning between executive, creative, and hospitality areas.
  • Selecting and curating materials, finishes, and accessories to achieve a refined, home-like atmosphere.
  • Leading the custom furniture design, ensuring every piece reflected the project’s bespoke nature.
  • Supervising execution, material sourcing, fit-out, and detailing to deliver a flawless finish.

The result is a VIP executive office that functions as both a workplace and a personal creative hub, setting a new benchmark for luxury private offices where leadership, innovation, and hospitality converge.

‍

‍

Name: The Pink Emotions
Top Section - Text #1: “The Pink Emotions” campaign was launched during Breast Cancer Awareness Month, reimagining awareness as an emotional journey. Instead of relying on symbolic pink visuals alone, the concept translated the stages of a woman’s healing process — discovery, empowerment, healing, and renewal — into a multi-sensory narrative across digital, retail, and live experiences.
Top Section - Text #2:
The creative direction used HAO’s luxury design language fused with pink identity to make the campaign both elegant and emotionally resonant. Every element — from visuals to activations — carried a story of transformation, positioning the brand not just as a perfume house but as a cultural voice of empathy and empowerment.

The Event with Jalila Foundation

As part of the campaign, Hind Al-Oud partnered with the Jalila Foundation to host an exclusive event in Dubai, uniting survivors, advocates, and the wider community. Guests were invited into an immersive, sensorial space that reflected the stages of the Pink Emotions narrative, while conversations and storytelling created an atmosphere of solidarity. The collaboration grounded the campaign in real social impact, raising both awareness and funds, while strengthening the brand’s role as a supporter of women’s empowerment in the region.

My Role

  • Conceived and directed the Pink Emotions campaign concept.
  • Designed the visual identity and communication style across retail, digital, and social channels.
  • Directed the activation concept and spatial storytelling for the Jalila Foundation event.
  • Oversaw product and merchandising integration (special edition Pink Box, fragrance storytelling).
  • Ensured the campaign aligned with both emotional resonance and brand luxury positioning.

The campaign created widespread engagement across social media and retail, while the Jalila Foundation partnership reinforced its authenticity. By framing awareness as an emotional journey, “The Pink Emotions” positioned Hind Al-Oud as a brand that goes beyond products to create cultural and social relevance.

‍
